The official romanization system for [Taiwanese Hokkien](/wiki/Taiwanese_Hokkien "Taiwanese Hokkien") (Taigi) in [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan") is known as **Tâi-uân Tâi-gí Lô-má-jī Phing-im Hong-àn**,[[I]](#cite_note-1)[[1]](#cite_note-2) often shortened to **Tâi-lô**. It is derived from *[Pe̍h-ōe-jī](/wiki/Pe%CC%8Dh-%C5%8De-j%C4%AB "Pe̍h-ōe-jī")* and since 2006 has been one of the phonetic notation systems officially promoted by [Taiwan's Ministry of Education](/wiki/Ministry_of_Education,_Republic_of_China "Ministry of Education, Republic of China").[[2]](#cite_note-manual-3) The system is used in the MoE's *[Dictionary of Frequently-Used Taiwanese Taigi](/wiki/Dictionary_of_Frequently-Used_Taiwanese_Taigi "Dictionary of Frequently-Used Taiwanese Taigi")*. Its main differences with [Pe̍h-ōe-jī](/wiki/Pe%CC%8Dh-%C5%8De-j%C4%AB "Pe̍h-ōe-jī") are that it uses *ts tsh* instead of *ch chh*, *u* instead of *o* in vowel combinations such as *oa* and *oe*, *i* instead of *e* in *eng* and *ek*, *oo* instead of *o͘*, and *nn* instead of *ⁿ*.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e3/20061014_ROC-EDU_Taiwanese_Romanization_System.pdf/page1-250px-20061014_ROC-EDU_Taiwanese_Romanization_System.pdf.jpg)](/wiki/File:20061014_ROC-EDU_Taiwanese_Romanization_System.pdf)

Taiwanese Romanization System

## Alphabet

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=T%C3%A2i-u%C3%A2n_L%C3%B4-m%C3%A1-j%C4%AB_Phing-im_Hong-%C3%A0n&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Alphabet")]

The Taiwanese Romanization System uses 16 basic [Latin letters](/wiki/Latin_alphabet "Latin alphabet") (A, B, E, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, N, O, P, S, T, U), 7 [digraphs](/wiki/Digraph_(orthography) "Digraph (orthography)") (Kh, Ng, nn, Oo, Ph, Th, Ts) and a [trigraph](/wiki/Trigraph_(orthography) "Trigraph (orthography)") (Tsh). In addition, it uses 6 [diacritics](/wiki/Diacritic "Diacritic") to represent [tones](/wiki/Tone_(linguistics) "Tone (linguistics)").

* [Palatalization](/wiki/Palatalization_(phonetics) "Palatalization (phonetics)") occurs when *j*, *s*, *ts*, *tsh* are followed by *i*, so *ji*, *si*, *tsi*, *tshi* are sometimes considered [multigraphs](/wiki/Multigraph_(orthography) "Multigraph (orthography)").
* Of the 10 unused basic Latin letters, R is sometimes used to express dialectal vowels (*ir* and *er*), while the others (C, D, F, Q, V, W, X, Y, Z) are only used in [loanwords](/wiki/Loanword "Loanword").

### Sample texts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=T%C3%A2i-u%C3%A2n_L%C3%B4-m%C3%A1-j%C4%AB_Phing-im_Hong-%C3%A0n&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Sample texts")]

Tâi-lô
:   *Pe̍h-uē-jī (PUJ) sī tsı̍t khuán iōng Latin (Lô-má) phìng-im hē-thóng lâi siá Tâi-uân ê gí-giân ê su-bīn bûn-jī. In-uī tong-tshoo sī thuân-kàu-sū ín--jı̍p-lâi ê, sóo-í ia̍h-ū-lâng kā PUJ kiò-tsò Kàu-huē Lô-má-jī, hı̍k-tsiá sī kán-tshing Kàu-lô. Put-jî-kò hiān-tāi ê sú-iōng-tsiá bē-tsió m̄-sī kàu-tôo, kàu-tôo mā tsin tsē bē-hiáu PUJ.*

Pe̍h-ōe-jī
:   *Pe̍h-ōe-jī (POJ) sī chı̍t khoán iōng Latin (Lô-má) phèng-im hē-thóng lâi siá Tâi-ôan ê gí-giân ê su-bīn bûn-jī. In-ūi tong-chho͘ sī thôan-kàu-sū ín--jı̍p-lâi ê, só͘-í ia̍h-ū-lâng kā POJ kiò-chò Kàu-hōe Lô-má-jī, he̍k-chiá sī kán-chheng Kàu-lô. Put-jî-kò hiān-tāi ê sú-iōng-chiá bē-chió m̄-sī kàu-tô͘, kàu-tô͘ mā chin chē bē-hiáu POJ.*

Hàn-jī
:   白話字是一款用拉丁（羅馬）拼音系統來寫臺灣的語言的書面文字。因為當初是傳教士引入來的，所以也有人共白話字叫做教會羅馬字，或者是簡稱教羅。不而過現代的使用者袂少毋是教徒，教徒嘛真濟袂曉白話字。

IPA (without tones)
:   [[peʔued͡ʑi (pi o d͡ʑe) ɕi t͡ɕiʔkʰuan ioŋ latin (loma) pʰiŋim hetʰoŋ lai ɕa taiuan e gigiɛn e subin bund͡ʑi ‖ inui toŋt͡sɔ ɕi tʰuankausu ind͡ʑiʔlai e sɔi iaʔulaŋ ka pi o d͡ʑi kiot͡so kauhue lomad͡ʑi hiʔt͡ɕia ɕi kant͡ɕʰiŋ kaulo ‖ puʔd͡ʑiko hiɛntai e suioŋt͡ɕia bet͡ɕio m̩ɕi kautɔ kautɔ ma t͡ɕin t͡se behiau pi o d͡ʑi ‖]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Taiwanese_Hokkien "Help:IPA/Taiwanese Hokkien")

## Computing

The [IETF language tags](/wiki/IETF_language_tag "IETF language tag") register `nan-Latn-tailo` for Tâi-lô text.[[3]](#cite_note-IETF-5)

### Unicode codepoints

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=T%C3%A2i-u%C3%A2n_L%C3%B4-m%C3%A1-j%C4%AB_Phing-im_Hong-%C3%A0n&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Unicode codepoints")]

The following are tone characters and their respective Unicode codepoints used in Tâi-lô. The tones used by Tâi-lô should use [Combining Diacritical Marks](/wiki/Combining_Diacritical_Marks "Combining Diacritical Marks") instead of [Spacing Modifier Letters](/wiki/Spacing_Modifier_Letters "Spacing Modifier Letters") used by [bopomofo](/wiki/Bopomofo "Bopomofo").[[4]](#cite_note-POJ-6)[[5]](#cite_note-jf-POJ-7) As Tâi-lô is not encoded in [Big5](/wiki/Big5 "Big5"), the prevalent encoding used in Traditional Chinese, some Taiwanese Romanization System letters are not directly encoded in Unicode, instead should be typed using combining diacritical marks officially.[[6]](#cite_note-8)

### Font support

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=T%C3%A2i-u%C3%A2n_L%C3%B4-m%C3%A1-j%C4%AB_Phing-im_Hong-%C3%A0n&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Font support")]

Fonts that currently support POJ includes:

* [Charis SIL](/wiki/Charis_SIL "Charis SIL")
* [DejaVu](/wiki/DejaVu "DejaVu")
* [Doulos SIL](/wiki/Doulos_SIL "Doulos SIL")
* [Linux Libertine](/wiki/Linux_Libertine "Linux Libertine")
* [Taigi Unicode](/wiki/Taigi_Unicode "Taigi Unicode")
* [Source Sans Pro](/wiki/Source_Sans_Pro "Source Sans Pro")[[7]](#cite_note-Iunn20-9)[[8]](#cite_note-10)[[9]](#cite_note-11)
* [I.Ming](https://github.com/ichitenfont/I.Ming) (8.00 onwards) from [Ichiten Font Project](https://github.com/ichitenfont)
* Fonts made by [justfont](/w/index.php?title=Justfont&action=edit&redlink=1 "Justfont (page does not exist)") foundry[[5]](#cite_note-jf-POJ-7)
* Fonts modified and release in GitHub repository [POJFonts](https://github.com/aiongg/POJFonts) : POJ Phiaute, Gochi Hand POJ, Nunito POJ, POJ Vibes, and POJ Garamond.
* Fonts modified and released by But Ko based on [Source Han Sans](/wiki/Source_Han_Sans "Source Han Sans"): [Genyog](https://github.com/buttaiwan/genyog-font), [Genseki](https://github.com/ButTaiwan/genseki-font), [Gensen](https://github.com/ButTaiwan/gensen-font); based on [Source Han Serif](/wiki/Source_Han_Serif "Source Han Serif"): [Genyo](https://github.com/buttaiwan/genyo-font), [Genwan](https://github.com/ButTaiwan/genwan-font), [Genryu](https://github.com/ButTaiwan/genryu-font).
